Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Continued To Dec-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl appeared by zoom for Carol Maloof (Respondent).
Kwixuan Maloof appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court asked the parties how they plan to proceed if there is no Trustee in place. Mr. Maloof stated that there doesn't need to be a Trustee and that he is proceeding as a Beneficiary. Ms. Lofdahl stated that she thinks it's a procedural problem, that Mr. Maloof has failed to provide a copy of the petition and that the matter should be taken off calendar. Mr. Maloof stated that the petition was filed on July 2, 2019, that a copy of the filed stamped petition is located in the documents and that the proposed order was filed on August 18, 2020. The Court noted that Judge Busch spoke with Mr. Maloof informing him that the motion for summary judgment was not sufficient. Mr. Maloof stated that he wants to take the motion for summary judgment off calendar, and that he wants to move forward with his petition and go to trial. Mr. Maloof also asked to bifurcate and dismiss the portion of the summary judgment from his petition. Ms. Lofdahl stated that the petition should go off calendar as it was never procedurally brought and expressed concern about how this is affecting her Client. Mr. Maloof stated that he asked to go to trial over a year ago and that Ms. Lofdahl has asked for four continuances. The Court explained the probate procedures regarding the requirement to deliver hard copies and gives Mr. Maloof one final opportunity to provide the copies. The Petition for Reimbursement of Benefits Provided to Carol, etc. is continued to December 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on delivery of a courtesy copy of the petition and a proposed order. Mr. Maloof is to provide said copies by November 30, 2020.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (5625 Summer Way, Unit 211, Culver City, Ca 90230)
Off Calendar Dec-27-2017 Continued To Jan-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for petitioner Gene Marchi; Kwixuan Maloof; Vyhyahn Maloof; Jim Chang (by telephone); Rob March (by telephone).

The matter came on for sale of the real property located at 5625 Summer Way, Unit 211, Culver City, California. Ms. Alavi stated that the property had been appraised at $487,200.00, and that the bid from the proposed buyer as set forth in the Report of Sale filed November 29, 2017, was an all cash bid in the sum of $485,000.00, which was a qualifying bid. Therre were no overbidders present at the hearing. Mr. Maloof stated that the beneficiaries of the trust objected to the proposed sale. He said that according to his research there were 14 recent sales of comparable or smaller units in the same building which resulted in sales at an average of $547,000 per unit, or some $62,000 less that the proposed sale. Ms. Alavi stated that the trustee had been required to compel one family member to vacate the premises, and found the property to be in deplorable condition. She stated that due to the limited funds available from the trust, it was decided to perform only cosmetic repairs and to offer the property for sale as is. She said that the property was listed for sale in October and two open houses were held, but that only two offers had been received. with the higher offer some $80,000 higher that the lower one. She added that the only contingency to the sale offer was a loan condition which had been satisfied, and that the buyer was prepared to buy the property with an all cash offer. She said that this was the best result the trustee could obtain. Mr. Maloof stated that he was speaking on behalf of all six trust beneficiaries in objecting to the sale for an amount of some $62,000 less that the average for comparable sales in the building. He dsaid that he had seen the condition of the unit when it was vacated, and that it was not in a deplorable condition. The Court requested input from the brokers involved in the listing and sale, and a recess was taken. Mr. Rob March and Jim Chang joined the hearing by telephone. Mr. March reported that the many repairs needed for the unit included replacing the dishwasher, refrigerator, carpets, light fixtures, sink vanity, electrical circuit panel and door lock. He said that the offering price was lowered to get more potential bidders on the property, and with hopes that the purchase price would be bid up by competing buyers. He said that the unit was listed at $489,000, and after listing the property on MLS and hosting two open houses, the only two bids received were for $405000 and $485000. Mr. Chang added that recent sales have been in the rangs of $487,000.
The Court decided to continue the hearing to give the beneficiaries the opportunity to file a written objection and to ascertain which beneficiaries joined in the objection, as well as to give the trustee time to submit a declaration regarding the sost for repairs and pictures of the unit in its damaged condition. The matter was continued to January 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The trustee was ordered present at the continued hearing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Wan. (Not reported)

Ms. Robello stated that her Client had no knowledge of what George Maloof was doing with the LLC's and that he can not sign the affidavit. Mr. Siracusa and Ms. Robello conferred about the equity line. Mr. Siracusa stated that he will accept the equity line and withdraws his objection. Fees will be paid upon receipt of the proceeds from sale of the Castle Vista Property.

Petition To Retain Landlord Tenant Counsel Regarding Property In Culver City, Ca And To List And Sell Property; Petition To List And Sell Property In Las Vegas, Nevada; Petition For Instructions Regarding Payment Of $5,000 To Conservatorship; Petition Regarding Rental Of San Francisco Property; And Petition To Authorize Trustee To Guarantee Payment To Institute On Aging
Off Calendar Nov-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Habib Bentaleb for petitioner Vyhyahn Maloof-Egson; Nancy Lofdahl for Carol Lewis; Leon Ainer for Kwixuan Maloof; Daniel Conrad for trustee Gene Marchi; Caroline Hinshaw, court appointed attorney for George Maloof; Lawrence Siracusa for Debra Dolch.

Ms. Lofdahl requested that payment of care of George Maloof be guaranteed payment out of the trust. Mr. Ainer objected to that request, as there are other trust creditors and limited trust assets. He stated his belief that the Culver City property is under water, and that it made more sense to try to rent than sell it at this time. He added that he had no objection to the petition to retain landlord tenant counsel. Mr. Siracusa replied that he believes that the Culver City property does have equity and can be sold. He said that if the property is marketed and there are no appropriate offers, the question of renting the property could then be considered. He stated that the trust does not have income sufficient to make a monthly payment of $5000 to trustor Dr. Maloof unless the trustee is permitted to apply rental from the 21st Avenue property in San Francisco. Mr. Ainer said that Dr. Maloof has not given instructions that the 21st Avenue property not be rented. He added a request that sale of the Culver City property be subject to Court approval. Mr. Der opined that it was unlikely the sale of the Culver City property would be likely to get overbidders travelling to San Francisco from Southern California. He added that this court would not have jurisdiction over the property in Nevada. Mr. Bentaleb at first requested an evidentiary hearing with respect to the the petition to determine interest in property and direct transfer of title, but stated that he no longer believes an evidentiary hearing would be useful and withdrew the request. Mr. Conrad advised the Court that the parties had attended a one day mediation in September at which nothing was resolved. Mr. Ainer stated that he is open to further settlement discussions or court mediation, but that some initial discovery needed to be completed first. The Court inquired as to what discovery was needed, and how much time it would take to complete. Mr. Conrad responded that he thought 2-4 depositions were needed, along with some interrogatories, which could be completed in 90 days. The Court directed that the parties aim for a settlement conference or mediation to be completed by March 31, 2017, with key depositions and discovery to be completed by February 17, 2017. Upon agreement of cuonsel, the petition to retain landlord tenant counsel was taken under submission, the amended accounting #1 petition hearing was continued to January 9, 2017 ay 9:00 am in Department 204; and all remaining petitions were continued to March 7, 2017, at 9:00 am in Department 204.
